steps.title: Match evidence to the claim
steps.content: Morales and colleagues concluded that snowball sampling may be superior to purposive sampling for recruiting rural study participants. A supporting finding must compare the two methods directly on the relevant outcome: getting participants with the desired characteristics.

steps.title: Compare the recruitment findings
steps.content: Choice B compares snowball sampling with a random baseline, not purposive sampling. Choice C discusses prior study participation, not recruitment success. Choice D suggests the recruited group differed from the initial recruiters, which does not show superiority. The needed finding is the direct head-to-head result favoring snowball sampling. The answer is A.
